content.full_text:
Law Offices of
LAWRENCE A. HAUN
Eleven and One-half South Washington Street
Post Office Box 1056
Sonora, California 95370

Lawrence A. Haun
Roger A. Brown

Telephone (209) 532-1958     Telecopier (209) 532-1963

December 11, 1990

Kevin Braaten-Moen
Technical Assistance Consultant
Fair Political Practices Commission
428 J Street, Suite 800
P.O. Box 807
Sacramento, CA 95804

RE: Confirmation of Telephone Advice

Dear Kevin:

     This confirms informal telephone advice you gave me on
December 6, 1990.  I appreciate your prompt response to my
questions.

     I represent an advertising agency which occasionally
provides services to political campaigns.  My client receives
fees and commissions from his client, a campaign committee.  My
client's contract provides for a standard agency commission on
all advertising created and placed for the campaign.

     My client retains various independent contractors to help
with the campaign.  These subcontractors typically perform tasks
which would otherwise be done by the agency's regular employees.
These subcontractors may provide free lance creative consulting,
media purchasing, or advice on creative or technical matters.  My
client wishes to share his commissions with his subcontractors.

     Commission splits will come from my client's earned
commissions, not from the campaign committee.  As a result, the
campaign committee will receive and report details of gross
amounts they paid for subvendor goods and services, my client's
commission, and the fees and commissions paid to subvendors by
the campaign committee.

     For example, my client receives a standard 15% agency
commission for television advertising.  He contracts with a
creative consultant and a media placement consultant to help
create and distribute the advertising for broadcast.  My client
may then pay a portion of his 15% commission to one or more of
his subcontractors.  The campaign committee will disclose the
full commission paid to my client.  Is my client required to
report the portion of his own commission he gave to the
subcontractor?

     You advised that my client is not required to provide his
client, the committee, with information on commission splits with
his subcontractors.  You also concluded that the campaign

Kevin Braaten-Moen
December 11, 1990
Page 2

committee is not required to report as subvendor payments these
commission splits my client makes with his subcontractors.

You stated that the Fair Political Practices Commission has
never advised people to report subcontractor commission splits as
subvendor payments pursuant to Government Code section 84303.
Moreover, the FPPC has gone further and advised people they do
not have to report these payments.

You explained that the commission has no advice letters,
opinions or other written materials which address these
questions. Therefore, I would appreciate written confirmation of
your advice to me.

My client wants to report everything the law requires. He
does not want to overreport where to do so would reveal
proprietary information and trade secrets. If competitors
discover the amount of my client's commission he shares with
subcontractors, they will gain a competitive advantage. If
employees learn that outside consultants receive a portion of my
client's commission, they may become disgruntled and quit or
demand raises. These are internal business matters the
disclosure of which serves no meaningful public purpose.

These commission splits may be made in other ways as well.
In some cases, the agency may write a check to the subcontractor.
In other cases, such as with media buyers, the subcontractor may
retain the commission split from the gross amount the client pays
for the media buy. My client may also wait until the end of the
campaign to distribute commission splits. In each example, it is
my client's money that is split with subcontractors, not the
committee's. Would your advice be any different in any of these
examples?

I understand your advice was based upon your examination of
the provisions of Government Code section 84303 and Regulation
18431, your discussion with Carla Wardlow of the Commission
staff, and your research of prior commission advice.

If I have misunderstood your advice, please let me know as
soon as possible. Otherwise, my client will follow your advice
until further notice.

Thank you again for your prompt and courteous help.

Very truly yours,

Roger A. Brown

cc: client
RAB:me:218.002
